channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe… — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today, some guy said to my friend >you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky My friend goes >wtf doe…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-04-22 22:24 UTC

OP

Today, some guy said to my friend
>you are lucky your parents are rich, not everyone is so lucky
My friend goes
>wtf does that even mean? How come your parents aren’t rich? Are your parents stupid? You make it seem like being rich is based on luck and not intelligence. Does that mean your parents aren’t stupid AND unlucky?
I have noticed a trend of the have-nots trying to pretend sons and daughters of rich parents “didn’t earn the money.”
Imagine thinking Barron Trump didn’t “earn his money” or Elon’s kid also.
I noticed the bottom 90% pretend it was just luckto make themselves feel better.
Like come on, even your parents aren’t not rich which means you come from bad stock.
Like okay, sure, “daddy’s money.” How come your dad is poor?
Thoughts, /biz/?

Early replies (first 3)

62144825
>>62144801
They're ngmi

62144840
>median HOUSEHOLD income
Why do they lie so much
A 22 year old in 1985 earning 20k is objectively different from a 22yo in 2023 earning 45k.

And median household income, at a time when people got married earlier is completely different from the median now where people get married later. So 22yos are actually even poorer, usually starting around 30k.

They lie so much it's no wonder people can't afford shit.

62144845
>>62144825
I swear people who say “daddy’s money” are poor. Like lets say someone is rich and has kids, would that person ever say “daddy’s money?”
It would not even make sense.
Like “mom’s basement” is another insult working class brokies use.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62144850
>>62144801
vote for open borders to add more illegals to compete with for housing and jobs. illegals obviously dont hurt the working class in any way ;)

62144853
>>62144840
What are you talking about?
The chart already shows the home price increasing even more.

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-04-22 22:20 UTC

BTC = Imposter

That's all folks. "Invest" accordingly...

62144729

62144745
>>62144729
artificial demand

62144782
>>62144729
BSV

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62144813
>>62144745
god lisa has the worst nose ever except for maybe hyoyeon

62144830
>>62144724
>Imposter
ESL nigger lol

item.title: XRP is the biggest scam ever — 8 replies — 14.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
XRP is the biggest scam ever

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-22 21:50 UTC

OP

they sell token to shift money over to the tradfi company Ripple to buy actual companies and assets and don't pay any dividends or yield on actual xrp tokens. I've seen actual xrp baggies say this is somehow a good thing.

Early replies (first 3)

62143238
Gee, wonder what they could buy

62143321
>>62143203
Means I can buy more for less. BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62143438
>>62143203
>ethereum foundation sells ETH and what
>Chainlink labs sells LINK and what

hypocrites and mind raped baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62143537
Biggest and longest running scam in crypto
I sold my ripple shares in 2017 and never looked back

62143560
>>62143438
>>ethereum foundation sells ETH and what
uses the funds to support their protocol
>>Chainlink labs sells LINK and what
uses the funds to support their protocol

>hypocrites and mind raped baggies
the other buys companies and ignores their own blockchain then gas lights their own community and claims that "xrp is the center of everything they do"

62143593
>>62143203
Did they pass the Clarity Act yet? No? When are they gonna pass it? Nobody knows? So, same as yesterday?

62143907
>>62143438
Someone post the alts chipmunk.

62144675
>>62143203
Seething linkbaggie
how is that flippening coming along zach?
https://link-flip.alwaysbeshipp.ing/

item.title: The biggest polish cryptocurrency is falling and you're laughing BTW, CEO escaped to Israel already. — 4 replies — 9.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
The biggest polish cryptocurrency is falling and you're laughing BTW, CEO escaped to Israel already.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-22 21:59 UTC

OP

The biggest polish cryptocurrency is falling and you're laughing
BTW, CEO escaped to Israel already.

Early replies (first 3)

62144366
>>62144093
who?

62144642
>>62144366
ZondaCrapto and its CEO Przemysław Kral, now probably Konigsberg or something similar.
A previous CEO dissapeared without trace, some people are saying he's chilling somewhere on the private island, others he's dissolved in acid.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62144721
Zondacrypto is a polish exchange.
https://www.bitget.com/news/detail/12560605375074

in the Reddit post a user alleges the guy has a Israeli passport, and because
of that is probably in Israel now!?
https://www.reddit.com/r/Buttcoin/comments/1sqz5o0/zondacrypto_and_the_mysterious_cold_wallet/
Connect-Sock8140

item.title: Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition — 80 replies — 27.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - Mumbai Warrior Edition

Open thread • Replies: 80 • Updated: 2026-04-22 19:32 UTC

OP

Hello my fellow PolkaCurry brothers. We are again here, for another thread where we discuss everything related to DOT, Mumbai (the city of DOT), curry and more. The last thread was a resounding success, we managed to keep it alive for literally 41 days. We got paid 11500 + 5*150 = 12250 DOT for the thread and are now living in a period of enlightenment while eating with METAL spoons. Brothers, we have truly reached enlightenment. WE, the Mumbai Warriors will strike yet again and collect generations worth of money !

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Early replies (first 3)

62115437
We are eating with our METAL spoons in Mumbai. No more hands my brethren. We eat with out spoons, like proud warriors.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62115624
>>62115424
>>62115437

Still gonna larp as an Indian?

62115793
>>62115437

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62121953
>>62121862
>>62121922

62122759
>>62121922
>>62121953

Chart looks good

But in the last thread weren't you shorting DOT

62123242
>>62122759
Panjali, be quite you fool (https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/61932834#p62069282). Do you want the Mumbai warriors to eat with their hands again? Or do you want to upgrade from our metal spoons to silver and then golden spoons? We, the Mumbai warriors, are painting the graph bullish in order to lure people in, then we strike with a x125 short. We actually been holding short open since 55$. The whole of Mumbai will eat from that singular short. Panjali, you have much to learn about the Vishnu ways. It's ok my son. I forgive you. Together we are strong, divided we are weak.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62124594
>>62123242
Way to promote a project then rug people

62125019
Didint read thread, i Will let My id decide if i buy some DOT when i get home

62125037
>>62125019
w4, E3, Wasb, and purple colour... Yes.. i see...

Not buying.

62125463
this is the most demoralizing dot thread

62126538
>>62124594
>>62125019
>>62125037
>>62125463
Good job my fellow warriors of Mumbai. Thank you for keeping the thread alive with your comments.

>this is the most demoralizing dot thread
How so my Mumbai brother?

>Way to promote a project then rug people
Yes Ganesha. We play the peoples hearts and then we stab them in their hearts.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62126677
Sol 8>297 Is 37X
Dot 1,15 x37= 42

62126931
>>62126538
Holy shit. Are you actually an autistic retard?

62126975
>>62126931
Kamal, are you getting upset again because your mother took away your metal spoon and ate with it? There is plenty of metal spoons for everyone my young warrior of Mumbai.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127244
>>62126677
Vrishank, that is really good mathematics. Education is really paying off I see. Very good. You might even become a doctor or a lawyer. Yes, you shall reach to the heavens of nirvana one day my young Vrishank. I, Vishnu Kumar, believe in you and so should you my young Vishnu warrior of the mind chain, my young Mumbai warrior, my young metal spoon owner. Soon, we will have silver spoons for everyone, then gold spoon and after that we reach the nirvana of diamond spoons. The whole of Mumbai will eat.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62127578
>>62126975
Yep. You are officially a retard.

62127673
>>62127578
Vrishank, did you just really say that with the intent you did? Is there anything wrong? Are you not feeling ok?

62128205
>>62126931
>>62127578
you are talking to a bot (and so am I)

62128210
>>62128205
BIP BOP BIP BOP


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62129040
>>62128205
No, its actual autistic retard

62129456
>>62129040
Impossible, look at the time stamps of the posts

62129518
>>62129040
>>62129456
No, it's actually Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131415
100$ end of bull run


Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62131658
It is still cheap right now. Don't buy my bags at 25$. You can buy them now at 1.28$. Don't FOMO in at 50$ guys.

62131731
>>62116681
>What exactly does it have that sets it apart from Solona and ETH?
Solana has a success rate associated with it's transactions (see https://solscan.io/analytics). That's kind of fucked up that there's a 10 to 20% chance your tx might not get processed, right? I've never seen a DOT tx fail. DOT doesn't even measure failed tx https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily&relay-chain=polkadot&chains= because there are none. If you submit a tx it'll complete within like 6s to 30s. The longest a tx takes is when you transfer from one corechain to another. So when you bridge (use a HRMP channel) between corechains. And even that never took longer than like 40s from my personal experience. It's bizarre to me that literally up to 20% of the tx simply fail.
Another thing is that SOL literally has more "vote" transactions than anything else; https://solscan.io/analytics#txs . And vote transactions are LITERALLY validator messages to keep the network running. DOT doesn't count that as a tx at all. 60 to 80% of the tx on SOL are from validators to keep the network running. That's insane to think about that you need more tx to run the network than the users use to execute stuff. People are stupid for thinking that SOL processes 3k TPS. More than half of that is just to keep the network running. It's a literal scam number to be honest. It's just to make the normies believe that the network isn't a scam. The number of transactions is completely fake in a sense.
And again, the funny thing is that 1 core of DOT will be able to handle more TPS than the whole of SOL once either JAM or NOMT hit. Maybe even before that once the 500ms Basti blocks hit. If you combine 500ms blocks + JAM + NOMT one core of DOT will be able to probably do anywhere between 2 to 5x more than SOL as a whole and DOT has LITERALLY 100 cores and this will also probably be scaled to more sooner or later. It'll sooner or later scale to about 300 to 500 cores.

62131777
>>62116681
>>62131731
The 300-500 cores are after the network upgrades to 1000 validators from 600 and after a few more future optimizations. But DOT can then scale further by adding more relay chains. So, currently there is 1 relay chain that connects all the corechains (while they also have direct connections through the HRMP channels) and this relay chain has 600 active validators at any given time. At some point DOT could scale further by having multiple relay chains and separate validators on each relay chain and then having a "relay chain" that connects the relay chains. Sure, this would fragment liquidity in a sense but you could still transfer assets between the relay chains in probably like 30s or so... So in a sense the fragmented liquidity would be a non-problem. DOT is truly scalable while other chains simply are not. DOT covered all the problems because Gavin used to develop ETH and noticed "OMG, this won't work out" and then he left ETH in order to develop DOT. DOT is ETH done right in a sense. ETH is just BTC with smart contracts and DOT is an actual solution to all the problems that plague ETH and all the other smart contract platforms.

62135297
100$ end of bull run

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62137662
Soon we shall reach the nirvana of 75$ my Mumbai brothers. Then we shall eat with silver spoons.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62139771
You are not the body, you are not the mind, you are not your emotions. You are the silent DOT in the infinity of consciousness. You are just a wave in the ocean of consciousness that forgot it is part of the ocean. For when rememberance dawns, the mystery is not solved. It is lived.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

62141372
My brethren, the time is nigh. We're break out. The wave of consciousness is breaking free from the ocean floor.

62143550
Any second now BTC will erupt into a storm that has never been seen before. DOT will soon start it's ascension to the heavens for 75$+. We, the Mumbai warriors, shall eat with silver spoons very soon. The time is coming my warriors. You still have to to buy a big fat bag of DOT before you get priced out.

62143825
>>62129518
>>62131415
>>62131658
>>62131731
>>62131777
>>62135297
>>62139771
>>62143550

This is sad. Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement

62144010
>>62143825
>This is sad.
My brother, the sadness is in your own heart. Not mine. You are destroying yourself.

>Also stop larping as an Indian and perhaps you'll get engagement
My brother, I am the one and only Vishnu Kumar. Born and raised in India. I am brown as the mud itself. There is no denying it. I am a true Mumbai warrior. I am a proud Indian.
Also Panjali, didn't people teach you that questions are fundamental for a healthy conversation. My young Panjali, I am not here for engagement. If you asked I would've told you so. Oh young Panjali. You still have a long way ahead of you. If accept, I let go, I move on and I forgive you young Panjali.

Thank you for you kind bump of the thread Panjali. I shall later come to your house and give you your share of the profits.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar, The leader of the Mumbai Warriors

item.title: >nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi… — 21 replies — 22.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>nofap >religion >stock tips >meditation >therapy >healing crystals >self-help books >pomodoro >PDFs >affirmations >posi…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-04-22 21:27 UTC

OP

>nofap
>religion
>stock tips
>meditation
>therapy
>healing crystals
>self-help books
>pomodoro
>PDFs
>affirmations
>positive thinking
>DCA
>crypto
>"self improvement"

Early replies (first 3)

62143060
>/biz/: Business & Finance

62143073
Different ways of coping. No boomber ever had to read the meditations or go to the gym

62143145
>>62143073
the boomers who did DCA have generational wealth now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62143155
>>62143145
Exactly. And young people are watching youtube videos about Stoicism and Carl Jung and getting hair transplants to deal with the real world.

62144238
>>62143155
Stoicism is cuck mentality

62144266
>>62143073
no, they just drank HFCS Soda, budweiser, sat their fat ass in a lazy boy recliner and stuffed their fat fucking face full of Doritos while watching the nigger felon league instead.
Most boomers are complete fucking losers who only ended up retiring just because of their time in investments, not because they were some financial genius.

62144272
>>62143054
Religion is the only thing that matters in this life. Follow that and you will be more content regardless of whatever situation you’re in

62144289
>>62144272
>only thing that matters in this life
You mean easiest way to cope with the meaningless existence

62144301
>>62144272
just as fentanyl

62144309
>>62144289
Exactly this. It works on most people, and why shouldn't it. The smart ones are the truly fucked. Because we have to live in a constant state of realization of the madness of it all, and most of all, the inescapability.

62144381
>>62144238
That's frankly a pretty retarded way of looking at it
Women have been unfaithful since the dawn of time and the only thing you can blame aurelius for was trusting somebody with bad faith actions. Getting assblasted because of every little thing is peak niggerdom and if rome acted like that (there are periods they did), the world would have been much worse off for it. Don't be le stoic and force yourself to passively take every loss, failure, and slight like a doormat; just don't chimp out at every opportunity because you think you can

62144395
>>62143054
All you need is Catholicism and the rest will work itself out.

62144435
>>62144381
>Women have been unfaithful since the dawn of time
>trusted a woman
Why he trusted a woman if you say women were always unfaithful dumbass?
Your statement is very incosistent, he is not to blame for trusting a woman yet you say trusting a woman is retarded. So why you defend him. Dumb. I really don't get the Marcus Aurelius dickride

62144457
>>62144435
That's not even what I said, but I'm not surprised you can't see past an argument and a mindset somebody else gave you. 'Women have been unfaithful for forever' is not the same as 'every woman is going to be unfaithful for forever'
I also dont follow stoicism, that macro's reasoning is retarded is my whole point. Defending against bad arguments is also not the same as 'dickriding,' but being brown will put you at a disadvantage for understanding that as well
>inb4 n-not brown! n-no u!
Then stop thinking like a nigger

62144475
>>62143054
>self affirmations are things people that made it do
I actually say
>i am top 10%
>i made it
Multiple times a day

62144478
>>62144457
>Women have been unfaithful for forever' is not the same as 'every woman is going to be unfaithful for forever
Good thing nobody wants advice about women from retards with such retarded statements

62144497
fcuk you OP I scrolled down and was accidentally breathing in when I saw that image and now I feel weird.

62144501
>>62144289
Only people who believe that their ancestors were shat out of a monkeys ass believe there is no meaning to this life.

62144519
>>62144501
Lol ok kid let's see if you gonna say that on your deathbed or if you get cancer. Retarded NPCs like you will never be saved

62144572
>>62144475
>top 10%
>made it

kek

62144576
>>62143145
a monk would live like this but without the faggot TV and gaming setup
that's what bothers me about these "Let me guess. You need more?" images, they're not based and ascetic if you have a faggot TV

OP

XRP to $800 incoming

You have been warned.

Early replies (first 3)

62141456
This would give me literally everything I want and that's the main reason it won't happen.
Not the powers that be, my female friends, nor the fates themselves want me to have these things.

62141457
>>62141454
Stay in your cuck containment thread, faggot
Herbs

62141458
>>62141456
Don't black pill.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62141464
>>62141456
If you already hold XRP this means you already have been chosen. It's only a matter of time my friend.

62141467
>>62141458

62141476
>>62141467
NEVER lose hope. We will achieve our wildest dreams.

62141554
How does 11 cents sound?

62141569
>>62141454
Interesting. I'd heard 589 some months ago. If it does what it sets out to then yeah even 1K seems possible in time. Pops has a bag. I'm thinking I'll be rotating gains into it since I can't put a significant amount into it right now.

62141576
>>62141554
sounds good. then I can load up my bags.

62141577
>>62141554
Like a good time to buy more

>>62141569
You did the right thing sir, please kindly invest all your money in the XRP as possible

62141906
dare i say...SO BULLISH XRP
BOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62142054
>>62141454
>>62141456
>>62141457
>>62141458
>>62141464
>>62141467
>>62141476
>>62141554
I'm gonna..... I'm gonna...
IM GONNA FXCKING BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62142257
>>62141454
You'll be lucky to ever see $3 in this life you retarded kike

62142285
before or after they dump the remaining 40 billion tokens?

62142772
>>62141457
Cope

62143167
>>62141464
>>62141456
>>62141454
ask any middle aged poor person in crypto and they hold xrp thinking it's gonna make them a millionaire

62143946
KABOOOOOM

62143997
>>62142257

62144327
>>62143167
What about middle aged upper class people?
My brother is a millionaire (from career and stonks) and he's the biggest xrp enioyer I've met yet

62144503
>>62141454
herpie xerpies can't stay hard
thankfully they make a pill for that

62144536
>>62143167
For me and my conservative estimates, I only think it can help do so if stuff I'm already in takes off first and then I rotate into XRP. We'll see.

item.title: 92000 usd is actually very cheap for a TRD Pro Toyota Tacoma. — 8 replies — 16.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
92000 usd is actually very cheap for a TRD Pro Toyota Tacoma.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-22 21:55 UTC

OP

92000 usd is actually very cheap for a TRD Pro Toyota Tacoma.

Early replies (first 3)

62142701
>>62142695
>t. poorfaggoto Toyota sarariman

62142707
92k depreciating goybucks is a hilarious overpay on a turbo 4 cylinder hybrid motor that sharts itself.

62143527
>tacoma

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62143549
>>62142695
40k u can get a nice barely used lexus

62143586
>>62143549
Yeah I would rather have two used GX460s than one TaRD Pro 4 banger. Or a GX460 and an ES350. Or a GX460 and an ES300h and save on gas. That's the proper place for a 4 cylinder hybrid - cruising in a quiet luxury sedan on a road trip. Not in your spotless $90k truck with a 2 inch factory lift.

62143798
Honda Passport Trailsport is superior in terms of quality and luxury, but if you go off roading and mudding then TRD is it.

62144513
>>62142695
she fucks that horse.

62144696
>>62142695
You slickers have no use for a Tacoma.

item.title: Insider info here and i'm only sharing it because i'm a greasy nigger who's made millions from biz over the decade so wh… — 36 replies — 10.0/hr

Not giving the name because I'm not a total fag but trust me lol you all fucking know it lmao
Just remember you were too retarded to buy it at these levels and 5k could of put you in the top 50 holders lol
that'll be 150-180k within the next couple weeks lol some of you niggers might remember or know my wallet even
if someone guesses it i'll confirm

So fxcking bullish.

62141145
im too embarrased even when being anonomous to disclose who got it right (ashamed to even buy it because of how many times theyve fucked biz in the past lol) but theyre about to pop off
sniff it out im not shilling it directly that's for fag third worlders to do.

62141155
>>62141145
avi it is then
thanks
>>62141084
>>62141132
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62141170
>>62141155
>>62141069

Digits leading us to the troof. I still have a bag of that dog with goggles coin from years ago

62141177
>>62141145
now this feels like ur trying to get people to buy coins they themselves think are scams

62141206
>>62141069
It can’t be avi, $5k puts you at the number 1 holder. And if he meant 5k avi that’s no where near the top. The only options are dash file Tezos or icp.

62141213
good night anons, wagmi

62141221
>>62141206
ICP is too big, 5k usd would only get you 12k icp https://www.coincarp.com/currencies/dfinity/richlist/

62141224
>>62141213
sleep well

62141228
>>62141213
And none of it was real.

62141281
Bro deleted it lol

62141324
ok bro i chucked 100 of my minimum wagebucks at the doggles, thanks/fuck you in 2 weeks

62142141
awwwwwwwwwwwwww yeaeah

62142161
>>62140986
RLC baggies still exist in 2026? I thought you all killed yourselves. They should put you in a wildlife preserve bro

62142203
It’s insane clown posse just admit it

62142232
>>62141044
Up 15% in the last 24 hours lmao

62143703
stixil humiliation rape ritual continues with new year of baggies, im here for it

62143820
Check out CHIP, at 11c right now....
Up almost 2x what it was last night.

Forget this BS faggot who won't tell you a WINNER, makes you GUESS like children.

He knows nothing, stringing you alone.

Get YOUR chips in CHIP, today!

item.title: Arbitrum revealed itself to be a CEX in chain form — 26 replies — 5.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Arbitrum revealed itself to be a CEX in chain form

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-04-22 17:23 UTC

OP

https://x.com/arbitrum/status/2046435443680346189?s=20

They had the power all along to freeze any address's funds and take their crypto. Can't even cope with "not your keys, not your crypto" here.

Early replies (first 3)

62138208
>>62138188
My crypto is on ETH, so its my keys & my crypto

62138253
>>62138188
All L2s are centralized, this isnt news.

62138290
>>62138253
This is a different story in that this L2 has the power to spoof your private key. That's NEVER in the discussion of discussing if an L2 is centralized. When L2 and centralization is discussed, it revolves around the ordering of transactions and who controls the nodes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62138320
>>62138290
Well youre gay? So yah.

62138408
>>62138188
Every l2 is a centralized scam

62138910
>>62138290
It's not the private keys they control it's the tokens.

62138917
>>62138290
>to spoof your private key
no lol. this is very different

62138931
>>62138910
>>62138917

So what is it then if I run a chain and can take possession of your ETH or whatever coin in your wallet? I know USDC can blacklist wallets, is it this at protocol level?

62138967
>>62138931
>is it this at protocol level?
Yes, they can move ETH since it is the chain's native token. Moving ERC20s would be more difficult. What they can't do is impersonate the address, i.e. they cannot forge signatures and pretend to be the private key owner

62139424
>>62138967
What else are they capable of doing that people should be aware of if the security council comes together to conspire against them? Being able to restrict your wallet and deprive you of your own ETH is a really really big deal.

62139448
>>62139424
No it isn't. it's been known for centuries that L2s were always centralized and now that skellyman denounced them there's no incentive for otherwise

62139494
>>62139448
The discussions of centralization were around node transaction handling. Not being able to control the the very ETH in your wallet. You are conflating things.

62139604
>>62138188
This is why I hold LUNC
the only truly decentralized chain

62139909
>>62138188
> executed a technical approach to move funds
kek, L2s are so worthless. ethereum should have never gone into that direction so heavily. it was obvious that the devs would never give up power within their own rollup.

62140324
anything kikes do is a scam

62140341
>>62140324
Let the goyim deal in the real world

62140513
>>62139424
They can do things that a typical validator set can do. This is like arguing that mining pools can conspire to fork the chain. Can they? Sure. Is there an incentive to do it? Not really.

62140566
>>62140513
>They can do things that a typical validator set can do.

And I want to know what that entails. If some entity mindhacked all the nodes in the BTC network or in the ETH network, they still can't take your crypto. All they can do is censor your transactions. To insert fake transactions and rewrite history, they'd have to go through the network to rewrite each node's history individually to recreate the blockchain. What Arbitrum did is out of the scope of what you'd expect from a validator set. So what can they do?

62140574
>>62140566
>they still can't take your crypto.
That's not correct. Blockchains are not immutable, they are democratic, and anything can change at the protocol level simply by changing the node software. There is an active proposal right now to censor Satoshi's coins, and if/when bitcoin decides to fork into quantum resistance, those coins will be de facto censored.

62140675
>>62138408
Eth is a centralized scam
EVM is a dogshit idea that only got traction because of its ability to be gatekept, controlled, and manipulated.

62141600
>>62140574
yeah but this is not what we're looking at here. they didn't fork the smart contract or anything like that. instead there seems to be a mechanism in place right now that allows the arbitrum conceal of gigafags to move user funds to another address.

62141625
I remember the chainlink schizos and how they believed arbitrum was also chainlink lmfao

62141991
>>62140574
>That's not correct. Blockchains are not immutable, they are democratic, and anything can change at the protocol level simply by changing the node software. There is an active proposal right now to censor Satoshi's coins, and if/when bitcoin decides to fork into quantum resistance, those coins will be de facto censored.

And all the nodes have to consciously upgrade to this new software version. At the very least there's still a democratic consensus at node operator level to accept such a proposal. That's still an acceptable level of decentralization.

>>62141600
>instead there seems to be a mechanism in place right now that allows the arbitrum conceal of gigafags to move user funds to another address.
Exactly. The only people who have access to this feature is the security council who WE have zero control or influence over. These guys are too weak to sing "Niggas in Paris" in front of their own mirror without breaking down due to guilt over patriarchal oppression and the lamentations of the oppressed.

(They're not on the council, but this is example of the folks in the industry)
https://x.com/PatrickAlphaC/status/2012634410764398795?s=20
https://x.com/va_joe/status/2042239851387290022?s=20

Are we to honestly believe that if the FBI comes slamming on the door, they'll be "decentralized" enough to reject an unjust order to move X user's funds?

What do in

62142780
>>62138188
This. BUMP

62142948
>>62141991
>And all the nodes have to consciously upgrade to this new software version. At the very least there's still a democratic consensus at node operator level to accept such a proposal.
Kind of. In practice, only the main hashrate contributors matter, and that is a vanishingly small group. The classic hardline thinking about decentralization just doesn't comport with reality for any blockchain anymore, and likely never will again.

62143434
>>62138290
Are you retarded?

item.title: If you had a remote 6 figure job how would you spend your free time assuming you usually work like 2 hours a day? — 21 replies — 4.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you had a remote 6 figure job how would you spend your free time assuming you usually work like 2 hours a day?

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-04-22 17:32 UTC

OP

If you had a remote 6 figure job how would you spend your free time assuming you usually work like 2 hours a day?
I'd like to spend the time productively, but it seems no side hustle/business will pay comparably to what I already make, especially if factoring the initial time where I make nothing/lose money, so it's like a poor ROI

Early replies (first 3)

62142878
Lift and cardio 2 hours a day
Cook all meals from scratch
Practice a language
Pick up a manual hobby like gardening or wrenching.

Congrats you're winning the economy in the current year, just disassociate about the whole wagie shift, improve yourself, live frugally and buy Bitcoin. I lost my fake work 6 figure remote job and I'm going to kms.

62142917
>>62142878
>Lift and cardio 2 hours a day
I do this 3x a week from home. Full body. Got a 4-6x pack and good chunk of muscle updating my program every 8 weeks.
>Cook all meals from scratch
Yep, I do this. All home cooked including the sourdough bread.
>Practice a language
Might do this + travel more
>Pick up a manual hobby like gardening or wrenching.
Not into this one though.

Still have a ton of free time and feel like I'm kind of going nowhere.

>buy Bitcoin
Any thoughts on MSTR? I feel even if you buy the bottom on BTC this cycle it's just a 3x in 3 years. With MSTR I would think it's a 6x. Got 150k or so saved.

62142958
I'd just live in a trailer and work there while developing a property. At a minimum its worth the perpetuity of the mortgage minus the land cost if you live there and never even sell it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62143028
>>62142917
Not your keys not your coins. I don't trust Saylor.

62143040
>>62143028
>Not your keys not your coins. I don't trust Saylor.
I'm just not sure it's worth thinking this when it means losing out on a historic 2x additional returns. I'd be selling anyway by Q4 2028 to Q4 2029

62143045
Why not try harder at your job and earn better opportunities?

62143050
>>62142853
spend time in hobbies and fitness

62143053
>>62142853
>it seems no side hustle/business will pay comparably
do something that doesn't revolve around money you fuckin NPC wage slave

62143138
>>62143045
Like what? My job offers time to work on-call, which I already do for like an extra $400 a day (usually doing nothing) and take up anyone else's shift that offers. Besides this there's nothing without taking up way more responsibility. For a while though I was jobstacking 2-3 jobs remotely, but it was pretty hellish with meetings. Honestly made more with crypto, but I lost of most of gains from getting greedy in memecoins. Tempted to keep trying to apply to jobs, but the market is horrible right now compared to before. As for crypto, tempting to just chill out while dcaing to BTC later and following any new narratives/early mania.

>>62143050
>fitness
pretty maxed for this without going the roid route. Been consistently working out for 15 years. I've hit my natty potnetial. It's really not that time consuming
>hobbies
Yeah, I mainly stick to this, but I always feel like I'm wasting my time. Like I was heavy into music production and DJing, but feel like a poser compared to the dudes making a living off it or feel discouraged when I see most successful at it are nepo babies who never worked a day job in their life. I then go back to trying to make more money to at least make a dent in changing my life (not waging anymore).

>>62143053
I grew up really poor, so its hard to shake off.

62143143
>>62143138
why does it feel like a waste of time if you've already got a good job?
do you really need more money?

62143150
>>62142878
i do this and have become so fucking bored

62143158
>>62143138
>Besides this there's nothing without taking up way more responsibility.
Yeah so there's your problem your scared of responsibility

62143190
>>62143143
I don't want to be in this guy's position >>62142878
>I lost my fake work 6 figure remote job and I'm going to kms.
I'm thinking it might be good to diversify my income just in case I lose my current gig. I'm kind of anxious it might happen one of these days. Luckily been 4 years strong at the same place now.
>>62143150
yeah, it's boring. Even playing video games more than 1-2 hours a day will get depressing. I try to be productive otherwise I lean the hedonism route with trying to fuck every girl I can off dating apps/social media.
>>62143158
Well, why take on more responsibility when I'm barely compensated for it? I work on average 2 hours a day for like 120k a year. If I take more responsibility for 130-150k I'd likely work 4-8 full hours a day, which is taxed at a high income bracket, so I keep even less of it. It's likely a bad deal. I also open myself as a target to be cut when there's layoffs. It's always the highest earning non-sales people who are first to go.

62143291
>>62143190
>Doesn't have anything better to do with his time, but is scared of commiting his useless time to more responsibility in his job
Your just playing yourself

62143332
I do work around 2 hours a day but you're still on call and retards often ask you bullshit questions or even just telling them to fuck off ruins your concentration so I just waste my free time on 4chan or playing games

62143334
>>62142853
Why do you care? There are no remote 6 figure jobs unless you are grandfathered in from covid era and you are irreplaceable to your company
>inb4 nuh-uh
Those literallywho 5-person companies that do WFH to save on office space are an extremely rare and irrelevant anomalies.
Also look into hybrid jobs. They usually pay the same as onsite ones. Even if they allow only one day from home that's a ton of hours saved every week.

62143347
I spend my free time with my kids
Its great, my son has a fit and present dad
Intense cardio in the morning too

62143388
>>62143291
>Your just playing yourself
Sure I'll just stress myself another 2-6 hours a day for no upside
>>62143332
>so I just waste my free time on 4chan or playing games
yeah pretty much me
>>62143334
>There are no remote 6 figure jobs unless you are grandfathered in from covid era and you are irreplaceable to your company
Pretty much me. Got the job in 2021 and luckily on a team where we manage enterprise clients, so it's not easy to just replace with someone else that doesn't have relationships with them or know their system architecture etc.
>Also look into hybrid jobs.
Yeah, I get offered these a good bit a few hours away in bigger cities. Likely will do this if I lose my current gig, so I guess it's not so bad.

>>62143347
That's nice. Wish you the best of luck. I've debated kids, but feel I'd have too much financial burden. Haven't met any wife quality girls who aren't NEETs/college girls studying something stupid like art.

62143421
I did have that for years and now it's gone

62143450
>>62143388
>financial burden
its not expensive
a dog costs more desu
you make the woman; you wont find her at 100%

62143476
>>62143450
It'll add up, especially when likely no jobs exist in 20 years and they'll be forced to NEET with you forever. That's how it is for my younger brothers and half the people I know. I'd imagine it'll be much more severe later on.
I guess it's more so of a responsibility burden and losing the freedom to be alone and travel etc when one feels like it.
In all honesty it's probably just cope. I just don't like doing stuff with no upside. Kids are more like modern pets for most people.

item.title: Let me just say that you niggas ARE NOT maximizing your investments. — 1 replies — 4.0/hr

OP

Let me just say that you niggas ARE NOT maximizing your investments. You could be making 1000 dollars a day, but instead you gotta keep wage slaving your life away. [spoiler]I may be projecting a little bit.[/spoiler]

62144851
>>62144847
Yeah I can't wait for orange nigger to change his mind and liquidate me

item.title: CLEAN IT UP WAGIE CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP — 73 replies — 3.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
CLEAN IT UP WAGIE CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-04-22 03:45 UTC

OP

CLEAN IT UP WAGIE

CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP

Early replies (first 3)

62119436
>>62119165
legend

62119443
Some people have almost no power over anything in their life. They do trivial evil acts like this because its their only thing they can do. Stealing stationary from work is a similar thing. His life is probably miserable.

62119490
>>62119443
Put the fries in the bag, wagie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62128026
i already told you i don't have a job
if this weren't a retard thread you'd have come up with something funnier but it's blatant vapid spam
this site is fucked

62128031
avoiding flamewarring with me to keep the post count lower & thereby keep the thread up for longer
like i said this site is fucked, everyone is way too self-aware and there are shills around every corner and the mod team treats it like a top-down rat maze with no engagement

62128032
>>62123557
here's another one

62128038
>>62119165

62128047
i'm tempted to just fill the thread with replies & see what happens

62128050
it would take hours & there'd likely be duplicate spam threads on the board within 48 hours though
makes you wonder what the mods are doing, or at least it should
>>62123557
>>62119165

62128085
>>62119443
kek

62128168
>>62128038
if you'd done a shitty mspaint trace job it would still have a soul, like pic related

62128292
>>62119165
>be me
>restaurant wagie in singapoor
>some mutt tourist think its funny to throw ketchup on the ground
>find mop and tell him to clean up
>hahaha no you are slave you do it
>call police
>he starts going off about first amendment and rights
>police gets agitatef because he doesnt do as they tell him, he argues like typical american
>they grab him to take into police car
>he starts fighting back, yelling about rights
>they taze him and beat him with police sticks
>take him to jail
I was kekking the entire time

62128310
>>62127025
Target and Trader Joe’s are can’t miss.

62130589
>>62125676
How did she manage to smuggle all that onto the flight?

62130590
>>62128292
Cope wagie. You still had to clean it up.

62130710
This thread reminded me that Kek Adams has passed.

62130818
>>62130710
Guy was such a betamale

62132756
>>62130818
Well duh, working in an office is the hallmark of betas everywhere.

62132767
>>62132756
He drew comics.
Well, you either work in an office or youre some low iq blue collar.

62132860
trickle down (onto the floor) economics.

62133126
>>62130710
>>62130818
>>62132756
weak niggas still crying about the man lmao

62133190
>>62119165
lmao americans are actually so cucked
if a customer does stupid shit we throw him out and tell him to stfu haha

62133420
>>62130710
rip

62133812
>>62128292

He was just trying to enjoy a succulent Chinese meal

62133862
>>62128038
This is an accurate depiction of the person in the image. Some people are so embarrassingly pathetic then act like they're some edgy Nihilist. You've only got this one lifetime to repent your wickedness before you meet God

62133918
Why is being in the service industry so bad in America? Like something about it is just so demeaning and people are so shitty and rude.

Being a waiter in some small village in europe would prob be chill and relaxed.

62134155
>>62119443
this, except i have complete control over my life and do it out of malice/mischief

62134162
>>62130589
You are allowed to bring any non-liquid foods onto planes.

62134229
>>62125676
What a nightmare

62137688
>>62121327
I hear the calling of all my addictions at once, fuck this is beyond demoralizing.

62139836
>>62133862
g*d must repent first

62141222
>>62125676
H...how do you tell basketball a...americans they can't eat lobster on the flight without being r...raycis...?

62141226
>>62127025
I literally fucked a chick worker at target AMA

item.title: Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah? — 16 replies — 3.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do you flex your BTC to your family or nah?

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-04-22 17:58 UTC

OP

Wondering if people actually talk about their BTC with family or just keep it lowkey.

>We never really know how long we have in life, so is it better to share it or keep it private?

Early replies (first 3)

62138671
>>62138453
I only have 1 so that's nothing to flex

62138680
People with more money than you won't give a shit and people with less money than you will resent you.

Keep your mouth shut and if anyone questions your purchases or your lifestyle, pretend to be in debt.

62138700
>>62138453
Keep that shit private, openly talking about how much money you have casually is trashy. Only discuss money with family with the intention of planning / sharing, otherwise they'll always have the thought that you have more to yourself. Speaking from experience with a good family, money brings out the absolute worst in people.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62139442
>>62138671
Okay, but what if something bad happens to you? You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC, even though its value could be much higher in the future.

62139556
>>62139442
>You still won’t be able to pass it on even if it’s 1 BTC,
What are you talking about kek. You sound confused

62139618
>>62138453
i make mom clean my room for 5 satoshis

62140241
>>62138680
Okay, I understand what you're saying about.

62141418
>>62138700
You also have a point. Do you keep it for your soon to be senpai or when you're married?

62141768
>>62138700
Bad decision

62141874
>>62139618
why so poor?

62142197
>>62138671
I can't believe people on this board are shrimps

62142226
Im still in my 40s and Ive got 63 btc. I'm taking care of my health and aiming until 100 years old. I will enjoy my money for the rest of my life.

62142464
I don't tell anyone how much crypto i have, if my gf asks where I got so much money from I tell her it was from saving it, not going to target to drop $200 because I'm bored.

62142867
>>62139556
I’m not confused. I’m just saying it would be a waste if he couldn’t pass it on in case something happened to him, and his BTC would just end up stuck there.

62142904
>>62138453
I haven’t even told my imaginary friend. Been holding for over half a decade now.

62143627
>>62139618
send proof or else.
